meta.title: JLPT N5 Kanji
meta.subject: japanese
meta.description: Learn 25 essential kanji for JLPT N5 — readings, meanings, and example words.

items.back:
ひ / にち / じつ — Sun / Day
Example: 日曜日 (にちようび) = Sunday

つき / げつ / がつ — Moon / Month
Example: 月曜日 (げつようび) = Monday

ひ / か — Fire
Example: 火曜日 (かようび) = Tuesday

みず / すい — Water
Example: 水曜日 (すいようび) = Wednesday

き / もく — Tree / Wood
Example: 木曜日 (もくようび) = Thursday

きん / かね — Gold / Money
Example: 金曜日 (きんようび) = Friday

つち / ど — Earth / Soil
Example: 土曜日 (どようび) = Saturday

やま (mountain) · かわ (river) · た/でん (rice field)
These are common nature kanji found in many place names!

items.question: 日 — What is the reading and meaning?
options: つき / げつ — Moon / Month
items.explanation: 日 appears in days of the week: 日曜日 (Sunday) and words like 今日 (きょう, today).

items.question: 月 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: つき / げつ — Moon / Month
options: つき / げつ — Moon / Month
きん / かね — Gold / Money
items.explanation: 月 appears in 月曜日 (Monday) and 一月 (いちがつ, January).

items.question: 火 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 火 appears in 火曜日 (Tuesday). 火事 (かじ) means 'fire (disaster)'.

items.question: 水 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 水 appears in 水曜日 (Wednesday) and お水 (おみず, water).

items.question: 木 — What is the reading and meaning?
options: きん / かね — Gold / Money
items.explanation: 木 appears in 木曜日 (Thursday). 木 alone means a tree.

items.question: 金 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: きん / かね — Gold / Money
options: きん / かね — Gold / Money
items.explanation: 金 appears in 金曜日 (Friday) and お金 (おかね, money).

items.question: 土 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 土 appears in 土曜日 (Saturday). 土地 (とち) means 'land'.

items.question: 山 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 山 is used in 富士山 (ふじさん, Mt. Fuji). やま means mountain in everyday speech.

items.question: 川 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 川 means river. 川口 (かわぐち) is a place name meaning 'river mouth'.

items.question: 田 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 田 appears in many Japanese surnames like 田中 (たなか) and 山田 (やまだ).

items.question: 人 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: ひと / じん / にん — Person
options: ひと / じん / にん — Person
items.explanation: 人 means person. 日本人 (にほんじん) means Japanese person.

items.question: 口 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: くち / こう / ぐち — Mouth
options: くち / こう / ぐち — Mouth
items.explanation: 口 means mouth. 入口 (いりぐち) means entrance, 出口 (でぐち) means exit.

items.question: 目 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 目 means eye. 目的 (もくてき) means purpose/goal.

items.question: 手 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 手 means hand. 上手 (じょうず) means skilled, 下手 (へた) means unskilled.

items.question: 大 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: おお / だい / たい — Big / Large
options: おお / だい / たい — Big / Large
items.explanation: 大 means big/large. 大学 (だいがく) means university, 大丈夫 (だいじょうぶ) means OK/fine.

items.question: 小 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: ちい / こ / しょう — Small
options: ちい / こ / しょう — Small
items.explanation: 小 means small. 小学校 (しょうがっこう) means elementary school.

items.question: 上 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: うえ / じょう — Above / Up
options: うえ / じょう — Above / Up
items.explanation: 上 means above/up. 上手 (じょうず) means skillful; 上 alone means 'top' or 'above'.

items.question: 下 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: した / か / げ — Below / Down
options: した / か / げ — Below / Down
items.explanation: 下 means below/down. 地下 (ちか) means underground; 下手 (へた) means unskillful.

items.question: 中 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: なか / ちゅう / じゅう — Middle / Inside
options: なか / ちゅう / じゅう — Middle / Inside
items.explanation: 中 means middle or inside. 中国 (ちゅうごく) is China; 中 alone means inside.

items.question: 一 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 一 is the kanji for 1. 一月 (いちがつ) is January; 一人 (ひとり) means one person.

items.question: 二 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 二 is the kanji for 2. 二月 (にがつ) is February; 二人 (ふたり) means two people.

items.question: 三 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 三 is the kanji for 3. 三月 (さんがつ) is March; 三つ (みっつ) means three things.

items.question: 百 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: ひゃく / びゃく — Hundred
options: ひゃく / びゃく — Hundred
items.explanation: 百 means 100. 三百 (さんびゃく) = 300; 百円 (ひゃくえん) = 100 yen.

items.question: 年 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.explanation: 年 means year. 今年 (ことし) = this year; 来年 (らいねん) = next year.

items.question: 本 — What is the reading and meaning?
items.answer: ほん / もと — Book / Origin
options: ほん / もと — Book / Origin
がく / まな — Study / Learning
items.explanation: 本 means book or origin. 日本 (にほん) = Japan; 本 alone means book.

items.question: Which kanji means 'fire'?
items.explanation: 火 (ひ/か) means fire. It appears in 火曜日 (Tuesday).

items.question: Which kanji means 'water'?
items.explanation: 水 (みず/すい) means water. It appears in 水曜日 (Wednesday).

items.question: Which kanji means 'mountain'?
items.explanation: 山 (やま/さん) means mountain. 富士山 is the famous Mt. Fuji.

items.question: Which kanji means 'river'?
items.explanation: 川 (かわ/せん) means river. It appears in many Japanese place names.

items.question: What does 大 mean?
items.explanation: 大 (おお/だい) means big or large. 大学 = university.

items.question: What does 小 mean?
items.explanation: 小 (ちい/こ/しょう) means small. 小学校 = elementary school.

items.question: What does 上 mean?
items.explanation: 上 (うえ/じょう) means above or up. 上手 (じょうず) = skillful.

items.question: What does 中 mean?
items.explanation: 中 (なか/ちゅう) means middle or inside. 中国 = China.

items.question: How do you read 日本?
items.explanation: 日本 (にほん or にっぽん) means Japan. 日 = sun/day, 本 = origin.

items.question: How do you read 今日?
items.explanation: 今日 (きょう) means 'today'. It's a common irregular reading.

items.question: How do you read 入口?
items.explanation: 入口 (いりぐち) means entrance. 入 = enter, 口 = mouth/opening.

items.question: How do you read 出口?
items.explanation: 出口 (でぐち) means exit. 出 = exit, 口 = mouth/opening.

items.question: How do you read お金?
items.explanation: お金 (おかね) means money. 金 (かね) = gold/money.

items.question: What is the kanji for 'person'?
items.explanation: 人 (ひと/じん/にん) means person. 日本人 = Japanese person.

items.question: What is the kanji for 'eye'?
items.explanation: 目 (め/もく) means eye. 目的 (もくてき) = purpose/goal.

items.question: What is the kanji for 'hand'?
items.explanation: 手 (て/しゅ) means hand. 上手 (じょうず) = skillful.

items.question: How do you read 百円?
items.explanation: 百円 (ひゃくえん) = 100 yen. 百 = hundred, 円 = yen/circle.

items.question: How do you read 来年?
items.explanation: 来年 (らいねん) means next year. 来 = come/next, 年 = year.

items.question: What does 本 mean when used alone?
items.explanation: 本 (ほん) means book when used alone. It also means origin (as in 日本, Japan).

items.question: How do you read 三人?
items.explanation: 三人 (さんにん) means three people. 三 = three, 人 = person.
